info.title: LMS API
info.description: Lead Management System API. Internal management routes require a Bearer token obtained from POST /v2/auth/login (Cognito-backed). External lead intake routes are open and authenticate via campaign_id + campaign_key in the request body.

servers.description: Development - External Leads API (open — campaign_id + campaign_key auth)

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Obtain and refresh Bearer tokens for internal API access

tags.name: campaigns
tags.description: Configure campaigns, participants, and status transitions

tags.name: tenant-config
tags.description: Manage provider credentials for tenant integrations

tags.name: users
tags.description: Manage Cognito-backed user accounts and roles (admin only)

tags.name: audit
tags.description: Query audit logs and trigger S3 exports (admin only)

post.summary: Login — exchange email + password for tokens
post.description: Wraps Cognito USER_PASSWORD_AUTH. Returns id_token, access_token, and refresh_token. Use the id_token as the Bearer token on all internal API calls — the API Gateway Cognito authorizer validates ID tokens.

post.summary: Refresh — exchange refresh_token for new access/id tokens

post.description: Creates a new Cognito user with a permanent password. Assigns the user to the specified role group (admin or staff). Requires an admin Bearer token.

delete.description: Disables the Cognito user account (AdminDisableUser). The user can no longer log in but the account is preserved. Use `PUT /users/{id}/enable` to re-enable.

put.description: Re-enables a previously disabled Cognito user account (AdminEnableUser). The user can log in again immediately.
Sets a new permanent password for the specified user. Does not require the user's current password.

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When true, soft-deleted records are included in the response

delete.summary: Delete client (soft by default; ?permanent=true for hard delete)
delete.description: Soft delete requires the client to be disabled in all campaigns. Hard delete is allowed only when the client is not linked to any campaign and has no campaign leads. Without `?permanent=true`: sets `is_deleted=true`, `active=false`, `deleted_by`, and `deleted_at`. With `?permanent=true`: permanently removes the record from DynamoDB.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, permanently removes the record. Omit or pass false for soft-delete.

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When true, soft-deleted records are included in the response

delete.summary: Delete affiliate (soft by default; ?permanent=true for hard delete)
delete.description: Soft delete requires the affiliate to be disabled in all campaigns. Hard delete is allowed only when the affiliate is not linked to any campaign and has no campaign leads. Without `?permanent=true`: soft-deletes the record (sets `is_deleted=true`, `active=false`, `deleted_by`, `deleted_at`). With `?permanent=true`: permanently removes the record.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, permanently removes the record.

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When true, soft-deleted campaigns are included in the response

delete.summary: Delete campaign (soft by default; ?permanent=true for hard delete)
delete.description: Deletion allowed only when status is DRAFT or TEST, with no linked clients, affiliates, or leads. Hard delete is allowed only if the campaign has never had participants or leads. Without `?permanent=true`: soft-deletes the campaign (sets `is_deleted=true`, `active=false`, `deleted_by`, `deleted_at`).

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, permanently removes the campaign and all participant links.

post.summary: Link client to campaign (always linked as TEST; update status via participant endpoint)

delete.description: Removal is blocked if the campaign has leads; disable the client instead.

post.summary: Link affiliate to campaign (always linked as TEST; update status via participant endpoint; returns campaign_key)

delete.description: Removal is blocked if the campaign has leads; disable the affiliate instead.

put.description: Requires at least one client and one affiliate before moving to TEST. ACTIVE requires current TEST and all linked clients/affiliates set to LIVE.
Updates QA plugin settings for a campaign. Currently supports duplicate_check plugin with configurable enabled flag and criteria fields.

get.summary: List base criteria fields for a campaign
get.description: Returns all criteria fields defined on the campaign in their configured order.

post.summary: Add a criteria field to a campaign
post.description: Appends a new field to the campaign's base_criteria array. Required fields gate lead intake — leads missing a required field are saved as rejected.

value.description: US state — abbreviations are expanded to full names at intake

post.summary: Seed campaign with standard base criteria fields
post.description: Appends the platform-standard base criteria fields (first_name, last_name, phone, state, email, ip_address, marketing_source, pub_id, campaign_id, campaign_key) to the campaign. Fields that already exist by field_name are silently skipped — safe to call multiple times (idempotent).

200.description: Base fields added — returns the full updated criteria list

put.description: Replaces the order of all criteria fields on a campaign. Supply the complete ordered list of field IDs — all existing field IDs must be present.

field_ids.description: Complete ordered list of criteria field IDs (CF-prefixed). Must include every existing field ID.

200.description: Fields reordered — returns the updated criteria list

400.description: Validation error — field_ids does not match the campaign's current field set

put.description: Partially updates a criteria field. All fields are optional — only supplied fields are changed. To remove state_mapping, send `"state_mapping": null`.

delete.summary: Remove a criteria field from a campaign

put.summary: Set (replace) value mappings on a criteria field
put.description: Fully replaces the value_mappings array on the specified field. Send an empty array to clear all mappings. Each mapping maps one or more raw input values (`from`) to a canonical stored value (`to`). Matching is case-insensitive.

200.description: Value mappings replaced — returns the updated field

get.summary: List logic rules for a campaign
get.description: Returns all logic rules attached to the campaign. Logic rules are evaluated after criteria validation to apply pass/fail decisions on incoming leads.

post.summary: Create a logic rule for a campaign
post.description: Appends a new logic rule to the campaign. Rules are evaluated in order — the first matching rule's action (pass or fail) is applied. If no rules match the lead passes by default.

failRule.summary: Reject leads that are not from California

passRule.summary: Allow leads from known trusted emails

put.description: Partially updates a logic rule. Supply only the fields you want to change.

post.summary: Submit live lead (EXTERNAL API; campaign must be ACTIVE; affiliate campaign_status must be LIVE)

200.description: Lead processed. The lead is always saved to the database. Check `rejected` and `rejection_reason` to determine whether it was accepted.

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When true, soft-deleted leads are included in the response

delete.summary: Delete lead (INTERNAL API — soft by default; ?permanent=true for hard delete)
delete.description: Without `?permanent=true`: soft-deletes the lead. With `?permanent=true`: permanently removes the record.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, permanently removes the record.

post.summary: Submit test lead (EXTERNAL API; campaign must be TEST; affiliate campaign_status must be TEST)

200.description: Test lead processed. The lead is always saved with test=true. Check `rejected` and `rejection_reason` to determine acceptance.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Filter by provider (e.g. trusted_form, ipqs)

put.summary: Disable a credential (soft-disable, does not delete)

get.description: Returns the canonical AVAILABLE_PLUGINS registry — the fixed list of all plugins the platform supports. No database call; safe to cache. Use this to populate a plugin selection list, know each plugin's credential_type, and show plugin descriptions. This list is independent of whether plugins have been configured or enabled.

post.description: Defines the credential fields a plugin integration requires. The frontend reads these to dynamically render credential creation forms.

get.description: Returns all stored credential schema records. Frontend uses this list to render dynamic credential forms per plugin. Pass `?includeDeleted=true` to include soft-deleted records.

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When true, soft-deleted schemas are included in the response

get.summary: Get a credential schema by ID

delete.description: Soft-deletes the schema by default. Pass `?permanent=true` to hard-delete (only allowed when no credentials reference this schema). Hard-delete is blocked if any credentials are linked.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, permanently deletes the record (blocked if credentials reference this schema)

400.description: Cannot hard-delete: credentials still reference this schema

get.description: Returns exactly one entry per canonical plugin in the AVAILABLE_PLUGINS registry (currently: trusted_form, ipqs). Each entry merges the registry metadata (name, credential_type, description) with the current setting state (credentials_id, enabled). Unconfigured plugins are included with enabled=false and credentials_id=null — the list size is always fixed regardless of how many credential schemas exist. Pass `?includeDeleted=true` to include soft-deleted records.

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: When true, soft-deleted plugin settings are included in the response

get.summary: Get the global plugin setting for a provider
get.description: Returns the global default plugin setting for the given provider identifier (e.g. trusted_form, ipqs).

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Canonical plugin provider identifier

404.description: No setting configured for this provider yet

put.summary: Set (upsert) the global default plugin setting for a provider
put.description: Creates or replaces the global default plugin setting for the given provider. Validates that the provider exists in the canonical AVAILABLE_PLUGINS registry. `credentials_id` is optional — omit it to register/enable the plugin without assigning a credential yet.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Canonical plugin provider identifier

delete.description: Soft-deletes the setting by default. Pass `?permanent=true` to hard-delete.

parameters.name: permanent
parameters.description: When true, permanently deletes the record

post.description: Proxies a validation request directly to the TrustedForm API (`GET https://cert.trustedform.com/{cert_id}/validate`). If `credentials_id` is omitted the endpoint auto-resolves the active TrustedForm credential configured in the tenant plugin settings. Passing `credentials_id` performs an ad-hoc validation using that specific credential — useful for testing before committing a plugin setting.

cert_id.description: Bare 40-char hex cert ID or full https://cert.trustedform.com/... URL

outcome.description: success = cert valid; failure = cert invalid/expired; error = API/network error

reason.description: Human-readable reason when outcome is failure or error

post.description: Proxy endpoint for running an IPQualityScore (IPQS) fraud-score check directly — without going through a full lead submission. Credentials are auto-resolved from the active 'ipqs' plugin setting in the tenant configuration. At least one of `phone`, `email`, or `ip_address` must be provided.

phone.description: E.164 or plain phone number to check

ip_address.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address to check

get.summary: Activity feed — cross-entity audit log query
get.description: Returns a paginated list of audit log entries across all entities. Requires either entity_type or actor_sub. Supports optional date range filtering. Results are ordered newest-first.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Filter by entity type (e.g. lead, campaign, client, affiliate, user)

parameters.name: actor_sub
parameters.description: Filter by Cognito sub of the actor who made the change

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return entries at or after this time

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: ISO 8601 timestamp — only return entries at or before this time

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return (default 50)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned as nextCursor from a previous response

get.description: Returns paginated audit log entries for a single entity (identified by its ID). Results are ordered newest-first.

parameters.name: entityId
parameters.description: The entity ID to fetch history for (e.g. a lead ID, client ID, or user email)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return (default 50)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned as nextCursor from a previous response

post.summary: Trigger S3 export for a calendar date
post.description: Exports all audit log entries for the specified date (YYYY-MM-DD) to S3 as NDJSON. Also triggered automatically by EventBridge Scheduler at midnight UTC. Admin only.

BearerAuth.description: JWT id_token obtained from POST /v2/auth/login. Include as Authorization: Bearer <id_token> on all internal API requests. Note: the API Gateway Cognito authorizer validates the id_token, not the access_token.

refresh_token.description: refresh_token from a previous login or refresh response

access_token.description: JWT access token. Note: use id_token (not access_token) as the Bearer token on internal API calls — the API Gateway Cognito authorizer validates ID tokens.

id_token.description: JWT ID token — use as Bearer token: Authorization: Bearer <id_token>

refresh_token.description: Long-lived token; use to obtain new access/id tokens without re-entering password

ClientStatus.description: INACTIVE is set automatically when a client is soft-deleted.

AffiliateStatus.description: INACTIVE is set automatically when an affiliate is soft-deleted.

deleted_at.description: ISO timestamp of soft-deletion; null if not deleted

is_deleted.description: True when the record has been soft-deleted

active.description: Convenience inverse of is_deleted: false when soft-deleted, true otherwise

edit_history.description: Ordered log of all field-level changes made via PUT /clients/{id}. Each entry records the field name, before/after values, timestamp, and actor.

field.description: Name of the field that changed (e.g. name, email, status, phone, client_code)

previous_value.description: Value before the change (null if the field was newly added)

deleted_at.description: ISO timestamp of soft-deletion; null if not deleted

is_deleted.description: True when the record has been soft-deleted

active.description: Convenience inverse of is_deleted: false when soft-deleted, true otherwise

edit_history.description: Ordered log of all field-level changes made via PUT /affiliates/{id}. Each entry records the field name, before/after values, timestamp, and actor.

field.description: Name of the field that changed (e.g. name, email, status, phone, company, affiliate_code)

previous_value.description: Value before the change (null if the field was newly added)

ParticipantHistoryEntry.description: A single audit record describing a change to a campaign participant (affiliate or client).

event.description: Type of change: 'linked' on initial or re-link, 'status_changed' when the participant status is updated, 'key_rotated' when the affiliate campaign_key is regenerated.

field.description: The name of the field that changed (e.g. 'status', 'campaign_key').

from.description: Value before the change. Absent when the field did not previously exist.

changed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the change occurred.

changed_by.description: Identity of the actor who made the change.

history.description: Chronological log of all changes made to this affiliate's participation (status changes, key rotations, initial link).
Chronological log of all changes made to this client's participation (status changes, initial link).

deleted_at.description: ISO timestamp of soft-deletion; null if not deleted

is_deleted.description: True when the campaign has been soft-deleted

edit_history.description: Ordered log of campaign-level field changes (currently tracks name). Appended automatically on PUT /campaigns/{id}.

field.description: Name of the field that changed (e.g. name)

submit_url.description: Live lead submission endpoint for this campaign. Affiliates POST lead payloads to this URL using their campaign_key.

submit_url_test.description: Test lead submission endpoint. Same as submit_url with /test appended. Leads posted here are stored with test=true and never forwarded to clients.

base_criteria.description: Ordered list of criteria fields that define the expected lead payload shape. Required fields gate intake — leads missing a required field are saved with rejected=true.

edit_history.description: Ordered log of all field changes. Each entry records what changed, the before/after values, when it happened, and who made the change.

field.description: Dot-notation path of the changed field, e.g. payload.name or payload.email

previous_value.description: Value before the change (null if the field was newly added)

new_value.description: Value after the change (null if the field was removed)

created_by.description: campaign_key of the affiliate that submitted this lead (actor identity at intake)

updated_by.description: Username of the internal user who last updated this lead

deleted_by.description: Username of the user who soft-deleted this lead; null if not deleted

pipeline_halted.description: True when the QA pipeline was halted by a gate failure in stage 2+. Always false when the lead was rejected purely as a duplicate.

halt_stage.description: The pipeline stage number where the gate failure occurred (e.g. 2). Populated only when pipeline_halted=true.

halt_plugin.description: The plugin name that triggered the gate failure (e.g. trusted_form, ipqs). Populated only when pipeline_halted=true.

halt_reason.description: Human-readable affiliate-facing rejection message produced by the halting plugin. Populated only when pipeline_halted=true.

IpqsCheckResult.description: Result of a single IPQS check (phone, email, or IP)

IpqsResult.description: Aggregated result of the IPQS plugin run across all enabled checks

success.description: true only if all executed checks passed

TrustedFormPluginConfig.description: TrustedForm plugin configuration. When enabled=true the cert_id submitted with each lead will be validated against the TrustedForm API. Use claim=true to also retain (claim) the certificate on successful validation.

enabled.description: Master toggle — must be true for TrustedForm validation to run

stage.description: Pipeline stage number (minimum 2; stage 1 is reserved for duplicate_check). Lower numbers run first. Plugins sharing the same stage number run in parallel.

gate.description: When true, a TrustedForm failure halts the pipeline and the lead is saved as rejected. When false, failure is recorded but the pipeline continues.

claim.description: When true, a successful validation is immediately followed by a claim (retain) call to TrustedForm. Set to false if you only need to validate without consuming the certificate.

vendor.description: Optional vendor name forwarded to the TrustedForm API during validation.

IpqsPluginConfig.description: IPQS plugin configuration. When enabled=true, any sub-check with enabled=true will run. All criteria defaults are applied automatically when not provided.

enabled.description: Master toggle — must be true for any sub-check to run

stage.description: Pipeline stage number (minimum 2). Lower numbers run first. Plugins sharing the same stage number run in parallel.

gate.description: When true, an IPQS failure halts the pipeline and the lead is saved as rejected. When false, failure is recorded but the pipeline continues.

UpdateClientRequest.description: All fields are optional. Only supplied fields are updated; every changed field is appended to edit_history automatically.

UpdateAffiliateRequest.description: All fields are optional. Only supplied fields are updated; every changed field is appended to edit_history automatically.

CreateCampaignRequest.description: Campaigns are created as DRAFT by default.

UpdateCampaignStatusRequest.description: Allowed flow: DRAFT -> TEST -> ACTIVE. Requires at least one linked client and affiliate before moving to TEST. ACTIVE requires current TEST, at least one LIVE client and one LIVE affiliate, and no participants remaining in TEST (DISABLED participants are allowed but do not count as LIVE).

UpdateCampaignPluginsRequest.description: When duplicate_check.enabled is true, criteria must include at least one of: phone, email.

DeleteResponse.description: Returned by all delete endpoints. `permanent` indicates whether the record was hard-deleted (true) or soft-deleted (false).

permanent.description: True = hard-deleted (gone); false = soft-deleted (recoverable with includeDeleted=true)

LeadSubmissionResponse.description: Slim response returned to affiliates on POST /leads and POST /leads/test. Does not include internal QA details (TrustedForm/IPQS results, edit history, etc.).

id.description: Unique lead ID assigned at intake

test.description: True when this was submitted to the test endpoint

duplicate.description: True when a matching lead already exists for this campaign based on the duplicate_check criteria

rejected.description: True when the lead was not accepted (duplicate, affiliate disabled, or QA gate failure)

rejection_reason.description: Human-readable reason the lead was rejected. Null when rejected=false.

message.description: Confirmation message when the lead was accepted (e.g. 'Your lead has been received and accepted.'). Omitted when rejected=true.

UpdateLeadRequest.description: Only payload can be updated; campaign linkage is immutable.

RequestActor.description: Identity of the user who performed an action.

role.description: Optional; allowed values are 'admin' or 'staff'. Defaults to 'staff' if omitted.
Optional — omit to leave role unchanged.

UpdateUserRequest.description: At least one field required. Provide role to change the user's group, firstName/lastName to update display name. Fields not included are left unchanged.

schema_id.description: Optional FK to a credential schema ID (CS-prefixed). Links this credential to its schema for grouping in the UI.

PluginSchemaFieldType.description: HTML input type used to render the field in the frontend form

name.description: Internal key that maps to the credential object, e.g. apiKey, username

label.description: Human-readable label shown above the form input

required.description: Whether the field must be provided

is_deleted.description: True when the record has been soft-deleted

edit_history.description: Ordered log of all field-level changes

fields.description: Ordered list of credential fields the frontend should render

PluginSettingRecord.description: Global default plugin setting — exactly one record per canonical plugin in the AVAILABLE_PLUGINS registry.

id.description: PG-prefixed auto-generated ID (empty string for unconfigured synthetic defaults)

provider.description: Canonical plugin provider identifier — the unique key for this plugin

credentials_id.description: FK to TenantCredentialRecord.id — the default credential for this plugin. Null when the plugin has not been configured yet.

enabled.description: Whether this global plugin setting is active

is_deleted.description: True when the record has been soft-deleted

edit_history.description: Ordered log of all field-level changes

AvailablePlugin.description: A single entry from the AVAILABLE_PLUGINS registry. Static metadata returned by GET /plugins.

provider.description: Canonical plugin identifier used as the URL param in all /plugin-settings routes

name.description: Human-readable plugin name for display in the UI

credential_type.description: The credential auth type this plugin requires

description.description: Human-readable description shown in the Settings UI
description.example: TrustedForm certificate verification for lead compliance tracking.

PluginView.description: Returned by GET /plugin-settings. Merges PluginSettingRecord fields with AvailablePlugin registry metadata so the frontend gets everything it needs in one call.

credentials_id.description: FK to TenantCredentialRecord.id — the default credential to use for this plugin. Omit to register/enable the plugin without assigning a credential yet.

enabled.description: Whether this global plugin setting is active (defaults to true)

EditHistoryEntry.description: Single field-level change record stored in the edit_history audit trail

field.description: Name of the field that changed

previous_value.description: Value before the change (null if the field was newly added)

schema_id.description: Optional FK to a credential schema ID (CS-prefixed)

enabled.description: Whether this credential is active. Defaults to true.

is_deleted.description: True when the record has been soft-deleted

deleted_at.description: ISO timestamp of soft-deletion; null if not deleted

edit_history.description: Ordered log of all field-level changes

BaseCriteriaDataType.description: Supported data types for a base criteria field

IFieldOption.description: A selectable option for a List-type criteria field

label.description: Human-readable label shown in the UI

value.description: Machine-readable value stored on the lead payload

IValueMapping.description: Maps one or more raw input values to a single canonical stored value. Matching is case-insensitive.

from.description: List of raw input values that should be normalised to `to`

to.description: Canonical value stored on the lead after normalisation

LogicRuleCondition.description: A single condition within a logic rule group.

field_name.description: The criteria field name to evaluate

value.description: The value to compare against. Omit for is_empty / is_not_empty. For is / is_not, may be an array to match any.

LogicRuleGroup.description: A group of conditions (AND). Groups are evaluated with OR logic between them.

LogicRule.description: A logic rule attached to a campaign. Evaluated after criteria validation — first matching rule wins.

action.description: Action to apply when this rule matches

groups.description: Groups evaluated with OR. A rule matches when any group matches (all conditions in the group are true).

UpdateLogicRuleRequest.description: All fields are optional — supply only what you want to change.

BaseCriteriaField.description: A single criteria field on a campaign. Required fields gate lead intake.

order.description: Display / evaluation order (0-based). Use PUT /criteria/reorder to change order.

field_label.description: Human-readable label shown in the UI

field_name.description: snake_case key used inside the lead payload

required.description: When true, leads missing this field are rejected at intake

description.description: Optional free-text description for operators / affiliates

options.description: Selectable options — only used when data_type is List

value_mappings.description: Optional input-normalisation rules. Each entry maps a set of raw input values to a canonical stored value. Applied before required-field validation.

state_mapping.description: Built-in US state normalisation preset. `abbr_to_name` converts "CA" → "California"; `name_to_abbr` converts "California" → "CA". Omit or set null to disable.

client_override.description: When true, the client can override this field's value after intake

affiliate_override.description: When true, the affiliate can provide an override value for this field

AddCriteriaFieldRequest.description: Request body for adding a criteria field to a campaign

field_name.description: snake_case key. Must be unique within the campaign.

state_mapping.description: Built-in US state normalisation preset. Omit to disable.

UpdateCriteriaFieldRequest.description: Request body for updating a criteria field. All fields are optional.

state_mapping.description: Set to null to remove the existing state_mapping.

SetValueMappingsRequest.description: Fully replaces the value_mappings array on a criteria field. Send an empty array to clear all mappings.

value_mappings.description: New complete set of value mappings. Replaces any existing mappings.

AuditChange.description: A single field-level change recorded in an audit log entry

from.description: Previous value (null for newly created fields)

AuditActor.description: Identity of the user who performed the action

AuditLogItem.description: A single audit log record stored in DynamoDB

log_id.description: ULID — lexicographically ordered by creation time

entity_id.description: ID of the entity that was changed

date.description: Calendar date (YYYY-MM-DD) — GSI partition key for daily S3 export

actor_sub.description: Cognito sub of the actor — GSI partition key for per-user activity feed

nextCursor.description: Opaque base64url cursor — pass as cursor in the next request to retrieve the next page. Absent when there are no more results.

s3Key.description: S3 object key of the exported NDJSON file

UserId.name: id
UserId.description: URL-encoded Cognito username (typically the user's email address). E.g. jane%40example.com
